mnemonics: REQUIRE-MNTS: Requirements Modeling Needs To Scrutinize (Rigorous Examination, Quality Understanding, User Involvement, Iterative Modeling, Engaging Stakeholders, Modeling Notation, Traceability, Scenarios)
keyPoints: The requirements model describes what the system must do without specifying how it will be implemented.
It uses diagrams and text to create a precise, unambiguous specification for stakeholders.
Common models include use cases (user interactions), class diagrams (static structure), and activity diagrams (workflow).
commonMistakes: Adding implementation details (the 'how') instead of focusing solely on requirements (the 'what').
Creating an overly complex model that is difficult for stakeholders to understand and validate.
